title: artifact-ui — How This Tool Works
description: artifact-ui is an MCP tool that turns a JSON 'system_spec' into an interactive, multi-view HTML diagram (Architecture, Layers, Sequences, Code Detail, Matrix, Components). The Architecture view supports toggleable filters (node kind, change status, edge kind, group — deselecting all of one type fades it, with cascading fade to a group's members/edges and a node's own edges) and an animated playback timeline that scrubs through any sequence as a traveling pulse, fading everything unrelated to it. This page documents the tool's own architecture at the system level (below) and, via the Code Detail tab, drills into the key rendering/persistence functions in server.py and the system_spec/ package, and the system_spec JSON format itself — i.e. what an agent actually generates.

nodes.label: AI Agent
nodes.description: Claude (or another MCP client) calling the artifact-ui tools to explain code, plans, or architecture.

nodes.label: MCP Server
nodes.description: stdio JSON-RPC MCP server + a FastAPI app on :8765, sharing one asyncio event loop. Exposes get_example, render_artifact, save_artifact, get_artifact_spec. Hot-reloads system_spec/template_loader/system_spec_examples/design_system from disk on every tool call (by mtime) and self-frees a stale port on startup, so code edits take effect without a manual restart.

nodes.label: system_spec
nodes.description: Core renderer, split into focused modules: validation, layout, svg_architecture, detail_panels, filter_bar, sequence_diagram, seq_overlay, code_detail, changes, matrix, layer_diagram, styles, assets (CSS/JS), and render (the orchestrator). Validates specs, lays out the architecture diagram (Kahn's longest-path layout), and renders all views.

nodes.label: system_spec_examples
nodes.description: Library of example specs (sys_microservices, sys_event_driven, sys_monolith, code_impl_plan, code_bug_fix, mixed_levels) returned by get_example, several with sequence step example data attached.

nodes.label: template_loader
nodes.description: Builds the render_artifact tool description (use cases, schema, vocab, sequence example/animation notes) shown to the agent.

nodes.label: design_system
nodes.description: Shared HTML page shell, CSS tokens, and the page_wrapper() used by every rendered artifact.

nodes.label: system_spec Format
nodes.description: The JSON schema an agent writes as the 'data' argument to render_artifact: nodes, edges, groups (optionally with a nested 'detail' for the Code Detail tab), and sequences (optionally with per-step example/example_before/example_after data).

nodes.label: Artifact Storage
nodes.description: Ephemeral previews under /tmp/artifact_ui/{id}/{id}.html + {id}.spec.json, plus optional persistent paths (e.g. this index.html + index.spec.json) written when 'path' is given to render_artifact.

nodes.label: Browser Tab
nodes.description: Opened via webbrowser.open() to preview the rendered artifact, with an injected Save button for ephemeral artifacts.

nodes.label: render_artifact handler
nodes.description: The render_artifact branch of call_tool(). Writes the spec alongside the HTML for the ephemeral /tmp artifact, and additionally writes both to an optional persistent 'path' (no Save-button JS injected there).
nodes.signature: async def call_tool(name, arguments) -> list[TextContent]  # 'render_artifact' branch
nodes.code_snippet:
rendered_html = renderer(data)
html = inject_runtime(rendered_html, artifact_id)
artifact_dir = ARTIFACT_DIR / artifact_id
artifact_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.html").write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.spec.json").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

persist_path = arguments.get("path")
if persist_path:
    dest = Path(persist_path)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    dest.write_text(rendered_html, encoding="utf-8")
    spec_path_for(dest).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

nodes.label: spec_path_for()
nodes.description: Maps an artifact's .html path to its sibling spec path, e.g. index.html -> index.spec.json.

nodes.label: save_artifact()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Promotes an ephemeral /tmp artifact (html + spec.json) to a persistent path, e.g. into this repo, creating directories as needed.

nodes.label: get_artifact_spec()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Reads back the sibling '<name>.spec.json' for a persistent artifact's .html path, so an agent can edit and re-render it in place.

nodes.label: serve_artifact()
nodes.description: FastAPI route serving the /tmp preview, reading from the per-artifact directory layout.

nodes.label: _maybe_hot_reload()
nodes.description: Called at the top of list_tools() and call_tool(). Compares the max mtime across all watched source files/dirs against the last reload; if newer, purges system_spec/template_loader/system_spec_examples/design_system from sys.modules and re-imports them fresh, so edits take effect on the next tool call without restarting the process.

nodes.label: _watched_source_mtime()
nodes.description: Walks _HOT_RELOAD_ROOTS (template_loader.py, system_spec_examples.py, design_system.py, system_spec/) and returns the newest .py mtime found, used by _maybe_hot_reload() to detect changes.

nodes.label: _free_stale_port()
nodes.description: Called once at startup. Kills any leftover process still bound to port 8765 (e.g. an orphaned session from a crashed/replaced server), so a fresh process never needs a manual restart.sh first.

steps.label: write rendered_html + json.dumps(data) to both paths

sequences.label: Hot reload on the next tool call

steps.label: compute max mtime across watched files
mtime advanced -> purge & re-import

nodes.label: parse_spec()
nodes.description: Validates the spec via _validate_nodes_edges(), reused for the top-level spec AND for each group's optional 'detail' block (its own id namespace).

nodes.label: layout_graph()
nodes.description: Assigns node positions using Kahn's longest-path layering, co-locating nodes from the same group within a layer.

nodes.label: render_architecture_svg()
nodes.description: Renders the architecture diagram SVG, tagging every node/edge with data-kind/data-status/data-groups/data-from/data-to attributes the client JS uses for filtering and animation. Takes id_prefix (for Code Detail sub-diagrams) and an optional overlay (the hidden per-step animation paths) appended before </svg>.
nodes.signature: def render_architecture_svg(spec, positions, id_prefix="", overlay="") -> str

nodes.label: render_filter_bar()
nodes.description: Renders the Architecture tab's toggle buttons: node kind, change status, edge kind, and group. Client JS treats 'all deselected' as 'fade everything of that type' rather than 'show everything'.

nodes.label: render_detail_panels()
nodes.description: Renders the per-node sidebar detail panel: description, signature, code snippet, clickable hyperlinks to peer nodes under Calls/Sends and Receives From, and (when node_sequences is passed) an 'Appears in Sequences' section that jumps the animator to that node's first touchpoint.

nodes.label: render_sequence_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Sequences tab: a dropdown over each sequence's swimlane diagram plus a scrub/play timeline (render_timeline_widget) and a click-to-reveal panel per step for any example/example_before/example_after data.

nodes.label: render_timeline_widget()
nodes.description: Shared scrub/play timeline control (ticks, thumb, Now Playing card) parameterized by target_kind ('arch' or 'seq') so the same JS (_tlGoToStep) drives both the Architecture tab's overlay and each Sequences-tab diagram.

nodes.label: render_sequence_overlay_svg()
nodes.description: Pre-renders one hidden (opacity:0) animation path + traveling-pulse dot per sequence step on top of the architecture diagram, reusing the same curve math as real edges, so the Architecture tab's Animate control can play any sequence over the live diagram.

nodes.label: render_code_detail_html()
nodes.description: For each group with a 'detail' block, parses a sub-spec (nodes/edges/groups/sequences) and calls render_diagram_block() on it — the exact same diagram as Architecture, just scoped — selectable via a 'Module' dropdown. This is the Code Detail tab you're reading right now.

nodes.label: render_changes_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Changes tab from a flat node list. render_system_spec passes top-level nodes PLUS every node from every group's 'detail', so a modified function inside a Code Detail group shows up there too. Only invoked when at least one node has status added/modified/deleted — not the case for this artifact.

nodes.label: render_diagram_block()
nodes.description: Shared builder for one full architecture-diagram unit (filter bar, sequence animation overlay, SVG, detail panels, legend), parameterized by id_prefix. Used identically by the top-level Architecture tab AND by every Code Detail module panel, so a module's diagram is the exact same component as Architecture, not a separate stripped-down thing.

nodes.label: build_node_sequences()
nodes.description: Maps each node id to the sequences that touch it (id, label, first_step), feeding render_detail_panels' 'Appears in Sequences' section.

nodes.label: build_animate_block()
nodes.description: Builds the 'Animate' dropdown (with a 'None' option first to hide the scrubber entirely) plus one hidden-by-default timeline block per sequence.

nodes.label: render_system_spec()
nodes.description: Top-level orchestrator. Parses + lays out the spec, calls render_diagram_block() once for the top-level Architecture tab, renders every other tab, and inserts Layers/Sequences/Code Detail/Changes tabs only when the spec has content for them.

sequences.label: Render the Code Detail tab (this sequence)

steps.label: lay out this module's nodes only
render the SAME diagram type as Architecture, scoped to this module
steps.example_before: render_architecture_svg(sub, positions, id_prefix)  // old: stripped-down, no filters/sequences
steps.example_after: render_diagram_block(sub, positions, id_prefix)  // full filter bar + animate + panels

nodes.label: Node
nodes.description: A box in the Architecture diagram. 'kind' picks its icon/color (service, db, queue, external, module, function, class, file, package). Code-level fields (signature, code_snippet, previous_code_snippet, file_path, line_range, status) add the detail-panel snippet, location metadata, and Changes-tab entries.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "id": "orders",              // unique, referenced by edges/groups/sequences
  "label": "Order Service",
  "kind": "service",           // service|db|queue|external|module|function|class|file|package
  "description": "...",        // shown in the detail panel
  "tech": "Go",                // sub-label
  "owner": "team-name",
  "status": "stable",          // stable|experimental|deprecated|planned
                               // or added|modified|deleted -> Changes tab + colored border
  "tags": ["critical"],
  "signature": "func ...",      // code-level: shown above the snippet
  "code_snippet": "...",        // code-level: syntax-highlighted
  "previous_code_snippet": "...", // code-level: enables before/after diff
  "file_path": "src/...",
  "line_range": [10, 42]
}

nodes.label: Edge
nodes.description: An arrow from one node to another. 'kind' sets its color/style (calls, imports, depends, emits, subscribes, reads, writes, deploys, owns, returns, throws, overrides, implements, instantiates). 'async: true' or certain kinds render as dashed. Toggling an edge-kind filter off fades matching edges; toggling a node off (via kind/status filter or its group) also fades that node's connected edges.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "from": "ui",                // node id
  "to": "api",                  // node id
  "kind": "calls",
  "label": "REST",              // optional, shown on the arrow
  "async": false,               // optional -> dashed line
  "protocol": "HTTP"            // optional, informational
}

nodes.label: Group
nodes.description: A labeled bounding box around a set of node ids. kind='layer' groups also become swim-lanes in the Layers tab. Other kinds (package, team, domain, deployment) are drawn but don't add a Layers lane. Toggling a group off fades its bounding box, member nodes, and any edges touching those members.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "id": "frontend",
  "label": "Frontend",
  "kind": "layer",              // layer|package|team|domain|deployment
  "members": ["ui", "client"],  // node ids
  "detail": { ... }             // optional, see Group.detail
}

nodes.label: Group.detail
nodes.description: An optional nested spec on a group: its own 'nodes' and 'edges' (own id namespace, typically function/class/file kinds). Each group with a non-empty detail.nodes gets an entry in the Code Detail tab's module dropdown, rendered as its own mini architecture diagram + detail panels. This Spec Language section is itself a Group.detail.

nodes.label: Sequence
nodes.description: An ordered list of steps (from -> to, with a label) between top-level node ids, rendered as a sequence/swimlane diagram in the Sequences tab and overlaid on the Architecture tab via its Animate control. Both support a scrub/play timeline that walks the steps as a traveling pulse, fading nodes/edges unrelated to the active sequence, with a click-to-reveal panel for any step's example data.

sequences.label: Author a spec with a nested Code Detail block

steps.label: from/to must be a known node id
give a group an optional nested spec
detail.sequences validated against this detail's OWN node ids — exactly how this sequence you're watching right now was authored

sequences.label: Iterate on a persistent artifact later
Edit code without restarting the server

steps.label: edit a file under system_spec/ (or server.py)
